text: All right!  I'm thinking!  "Let me not to the marriage of true minds Admit impediments; love is not love Which alters when it alteration finds Or bends with the remover to remove."
en: All right!  I'm thinking!  "Let me not to the marriage of true minds Admit impediments; love is not love Which alters when it alteration finds Or bends with the remover to remove."

text: "O, no, it is an ever-fixed mark That looks on tempests and is never shaken; It is the star to every wandering bark whose worth's unknown, although his height be taken."
en: "O, no, it is an ever-fixed mark That looks on tempests and is never shaken; It is the star to every wandering bark whose worth's unknown, although his height be taken."

text: This is better than sex any day.  This is romance!
en: This is better than sex any day.  This is romance!

text: what?!
en: what?!

text: No one, sir.  It was just me.  I did the proofing so I inserted my article in place of Rob Crane's.
en: No one, sir.  It was just me.  I did the proofing so I inserted my article in place of Rob Crane's.

text: Mr. Dalton, if you think you're the first to try to get thrown out of this school, think again.  Others have had similar actions and they have failed just as surely as you will fail.  Bend over and grab your shins.
en: Mr. Dalton, if you think you're the first to try to get thrown out of this school, think again.  Others have had similar actions and they have failed just as surely as you will fail.  Bend over and grab your shins.

text: Yes... sir.
en: Yes... sir.

text: What is this "Dead Potts Society"?  I want names.
en: What is this "Dead Potts Society"?  I want names.

text: It's only me, Mr. Nolan.  I swear. I made it up.
en: It's only me, Mr. Nolan.  I swear. I made it up.

text: If I find that there are others, Mr. Dalton, they will be expelled and you will remain enrolled.  Stand up.
en: If I find that there are others, Mr. Dalton, they will be expelled and you will remain enrolled.  Stand up.

text: No.
en: No.

text: What happened?
en: What happened?

text: I'm supposed to turn everybody in, apologize to the school and all will be forgiven.
en: I'm supposed to turn everybody in, apologize to the school and all will be forgiven.

text: Damn it, Neil, the name is Nuwanda.
en: Damn it, Neil, the name is Nuwanda.

text: I didn't know you taught.
en: I didn't know you taught.

text: English.  Way before your time.  It was hard giving it up, I'll tell you.  I'm hearing rumors, John, of some unusual teaching methods in your classroom.  I'm not saying they have anything to do with the Dalton boy's outburst, but I don't think I have to warn you that boys his age are very impressionable.
en: English.  Way before your time.  It was hard giving it up, I'll tell you.  I'm hearing rumors, John, of some unusual teaching methods in your classroom.  I'm not saying they have anything to do with the Dalton boy's outburst, but I don't think I have to warn you that boys his age are very impressionable.

text: Your reprimand made quite an impression I'm sure.
en: Your reprimand made quite an impression I'm sure.

text: What was going on in the courtyard the other day?
en: What was going on in the courtyard the other day?

text: Courtyard?
en: Courtyard?

text: Boys marching.  Clapping in unison.
en: Boys marching.  Clapping in unison.

text: Oh that. That was an exercise to prove a point.  About the evils of conformity.
en: Oh that. That was an exercise to prove a point.  About the evils of conformity.

text: John, the curriculum here is set.  It's proven.  It works.  If you question it, what's to prevent them from doing the same?
en: John, the curriculum here is set.  It's proven.  It works.  If you question it, what's to prevent them from doing the same?

text: I always thought education was learning to think for yourself.
en: I always thought education was learning to think for yourself.

text: At these boys' age? Not on your life! Tradition, John.  Discipline.  Prepare them for college, and the rest will take care of itself.
en: At these boys' age? Not on your life! Tradition, John.  Discipline.  Prepare them for college, and the rest will take care of itself.

text: I don't know what misguided impulse caused you to pull that ridiculous stunt, Mr. Dalton, but, whatever it was, I hope you've learned your lesson.
en: I don't know what misguided impulse caused you to pull that ridiculous stunt, Mr. Dalton, but, whatever it was, I hope you've learned your lesson.

text: You're siding with Mr. Nolan?!  What about carpe diem and sucking all the marrow out of life and all that?
en: You're siding with Mr. Nolan?!  What about carpe diem and sucking all the marrow out of life and all that?

text: Sucking out the marrow doesn't mean getting the bone stuck in your throat, Charles.  You still have responsibilities to yourself and those who care about you.
en: Sucking out the marrow doesn't mean getting the bone stuck in your throat, Charles.  You still have responsibilities to yourself and those who care about you.

text: But I thought-
en: But I thought-

text: Like, if nothing else, the opportunity to attend my classes, understand?
en: Like, if nothing else, the opportunity to attend my classes, understand?

text: Yes sir.
en: Yes sir.

text: So keep your head about you--the lot of you--understood?
en: So keep your head about you--the lot of you--understood?

text: Rahesh Non never existed, Mr. Meeks. You make him or someone like him up.  No self important college professor such as this one would dare admit ignorance of such an obviously important figure and you will probably receive a comment similar to the one I received:
en: Rahesh Non never existed, Mr. Meeks. You make him or someone like him up.  No self important college professor such as this one would dare admit ignorance of such an obviously important figure and you will probably receive a comment similar to the one I received:

text: I will be with thee straight.
en: I will be with thee straight.

text: Follow me then to plainer ground.  God, I love this!
en: Follow me then to plainer ground.  God, I love this!

text: This play?
en: This play?

text: Yes, and acting!  It's got to be one of the most wonderful things in the world. Most people, if they're lucky, live about half an exciting life! If I could get the parts, I could live dozens of lives.
en: Yes, and acting!  It's got to be one of the most wonderful things in the world. Most people, if they're lucky, live about half an exciting life! If I could get the parts, I could live dozens of lives.

text: No thanks.
en: No thanks.

text: Lots of girls.  The girl who plays Hermia is incredible.
en: Lots of girls.  The girl who plays Hermia is incredible.

text: I'll come to the performance.
en: I'll come to the performance.

text: Chicken shit.  Where were we?
en: Chicken shit.  Where were we?

text: Yea, art thou there?
en: Yea, art thou there?

text: Put more into it!
en: Put more into it!

text: YEA, ART THOU THERE?!
en: YEA, ART THOU THERE?!

text: That's it!  "Follow my voice.  We'll try no manhood here."  See you at dinner.
en: That's it!  "Follow my voice.  We'll try no manhood here."  See you at dinner.

text: Neil, you are going to quit this ridiculous play immediately.
en: Neil, you are going to quit this ridiculous play immediately.

text: Father, I--
en: Father, I--

text: Nobody-  I thought I'd surprise you. I've got all As and-
en: Nobody-  I thought I'd surprise you. I've got all As and-

text: Did you really think I wouldn't find out?!  "My niece is in a play with your son," Mrs. Marks says.  "You must be mistaken," I say.  "My son isn't in a play."  You made a liar out of me, Neil! Now you will go tomorrow and tell them you are quitting.
en: Did you really think I wouldn't find out?!  "My niece is in a play with your son," Mrs. Marks says.  "You must be mistaken," I say.  "My son isn't in a play."  You made a liar out of me, Neil! Now you will go tomorrow and tell them you are quitting.

text: Father, I have the main part.  The performance is tomorrow night.  Father, please.
en: Father, I have the main part.  The performance is tomorrow night.  Father, please.

text: I don't care if the world is coming to an end tomorrow night, you are through with that play!  Is that clear?  Is that clear!
en: I don't care if the world is coming to an end tomorrow night, you are through with that play!  Is that clear?  Is that clear!

text: Yes sir.
en: Yes sir.

text: What is wrong with old habits, Mr. Overstreet?
en: What is wrong with old habits, Mr. Overstreet?

text: They perpetuate mechanical living, sir. They limit your mind.
en: They perpetuate mechanical living, sir. They limit your mind.

text: Mr. Overstreet, I suggest you worry less about breaking old habits and more about developing good study habits.  Do you understand?
en: Mr. Overstreet, I suggest you worry less about breaking old habits and more about developing good study habits.  Do you understand?

text: Yes sir.
en: Yes sir.

text: That goes for all of you.  Now eat with your correct hands.
en: That goes for all of you.  Now eat with your correct hands.

text: Do you have to quit the play?
en: Do you have to quit the play?

text: I don't know.
en: I don't know.

text: What good will that do?
en: What good will that do?

text: Maybe he'll have some advice.  Maybe he'll even talk to your father.
en: Maybe he'll have some advice.  Maybe he'll even talk to your father.

text: Are you kidding?  Don't be ridiculous.
en: Are you kidding?  Don't be ridiculous.

text: It's better than doing nothing.
en: It's better than doing nothing.

text: I'm sorry.  I, we
en: I'm sorry.  I, we

text: Okay.
en: Okay.

text: Actually, I'd like to talk to you alone.
en: Actually, I'd like to talk to you alone.

text: I couldn't stop myself.
en: I couldn't stop myself.

text: Maybe they don't want worldly things distracting me from my teaching.
en: Maybe they don't want worldly things distracting me from my teaching.

text: Why do you do it?  I mean, with all this seize-the-day business, I'd have thought you'd be out seeing the world or something?
en: Why do you do it?  I mean, with all this seize-the-day business, I'd have thought you'd be out seeing the world or something?

text: Ah, but I am seeing the world, Neil. The new world.  Seeing a student like you take root and bloom.  It's worth everything.  That's why I came back here. A place like this needs at least one teacher like me.  Did you come here to talk about my teaching?
en: Ah, but I am seeing the world, Neil. The new world.  Seeing a student like you take root and bloom.  It's worth everything.  That's why I came back here. A place like this needs at least one teacher like me.  Did you come here to talk about my teaching?

text: Mr. Keating, my father is making me quit the play at Henley Hall.  When I think about carpe diem and all that, I feel like I'm in prison!  I mean, I can see his point.  We're not a rich family like Charlie's.  But he's planned the rest of my life for me and he's never even asked me what I want!
en: Mr. Keating, my father is making me quit the play at Henley Hall.  When I think about carpe diem and all that, I feel like I'm in prison!  I mean, I can see his point.  We're not a rich family like Charlie's.  But he's planned the rest of my life for me and he's never even asked me what I want!

text: You can't live a life for someone else, Neil.  You can only live for yourself. Have you told your father what you just told me?  Have you shown him your passion about acting?
en: You can't live a life for someone else, Neil.  You can only live for yourself. Have you told your father what you just told me?  Have you shown him your passion about acting?

text: Are you kidding?  He'd kill me!
en: Are you kidding?  He'd kill me!

text: Then you're playing a part for him too, aren't you?  A dangerously self- destructive one.
en: Then you're playing a part for him too, aren't you?  A dangerously self- destructive one.

text: I know what he'll say.  He'll say that acting is just a whim and that it's frivolous and that I should forget about it.  He'll tell me how they're counting on me and to put it out of my mind "for my own good."
en: I know what he'll say.  He'll say that acting is just a whim and that it's frivolous and that I should forget about it.  He'll tell me how they're counting on me and to put it out of my mind "for my own good."

text: Well, if it's more than a whim, then you'll have to prove that to him.  You'll have to show him with your passion and commitment that it's what you really want to do.  If that doesn't work, at least by then you'll be eighteen and able to do what you want.
en: Well, if it's more than a whim, then you'll have to prove that to him.  You'll have to show him with your passion and commitment that it's what you really want to do.  If that doesn't work, at least by then you'll be eighteen and able to do what you want.

text: Eighteen!  That's two years!  What about the play?  The performance is tomorrow night!
en: Eighteen!  That's two years!  What about the play?  The performance is tomorrow night!

text: Give your father the benefit of the doubt.  Talk to him.  Let him see who you are.
en: Give your father the benefit of the doubt.  Talk to him.  Let him see who you are.

text: Isn't there an easier way?
en: Isn't there an easier way?

text: Not if you're going to stay true to yourself.
en: Not if you're going to stay true to yourself.

text: Why don't you read it to her? It worked for Nuwanda.
en: Why don't you read it to her? It worked for Nuwanda.

text: She won't even see me, Pitts.
en: She won't even see me, Pitts.

text: Nuwanda recited poetry to Gloria and she jumped all over him... right, Nuwanda?
en: Nuwanda recited poetry to Gloria and she jumped all over him... right, Nuwanda?

text: Knox!  what are you doing here?
en: Knox!  what are you doing here?

text: I don't care.  I love you, Chris.  You deserve better than Chet and I'm it. Please accept these.
en: I don't care.  I love you, Chris.  You deserve better than Chet and I'm it. Please accept these.

text: Knox, you're crazy.
en: Knox, you're crazy.

text: All I'm asking you to do is listen.  "The heavens made a girl named Chris, With hair and skin of gold To touch her would be paradise To kiss her glory untold."
en: All I'm asking you to do is listen.  "The heavens made a girl named Chris, With hair and skin of gold To touch her would be paradise To kiss her glory untold."

text: Yep.
en: Yep.

text: I don't know.
en: I don't know.

text: I'll tell you later.
en: I'll tell you later.

text: Yeah.
en: Yeah.

text: Really?  You told your father what you told me?  You let him see your passion for acting?
en: Really?  You told your father what you told me?  You let him see your passion for acting?

text: Yeah.  He didn't like it one bit but at least he's letting me stay in the play. Of course, he won't be able to come. He'll be in Chicago on business.  But I think he's gonna let me stay with acting.  As long as I keep my grades up.
en: Yeah.  He didn't like it one bit but at least he's letting me stay in the play. Of course, he won't be able to come. He'll be in Chicago on business.  But I think he's gonna let me stay with acting.  As long as I keep my grades up.

text: So much the better.
en: So much the better.

text: Knox, why are you doing this to me?
en: Knox, why are you doing this to me?

text: You can't be in here.
en: You can't be in here.

text: Oh, but it's fine for you to come barging into my school and make a complete fool out of me?
en: Oh, but it's fine for you to come barging into my school and make a complete fool out of me?

text: I didn't mean to make a fool of you.
en: I didn't mean to make a fool of you.

text: Well, you did!  Chet found out and he's nuts.  It took everything I could do to keep him from coming here and killing you. You have to stop this stuff, Knox.
en: Well, you did!  Chet found out and he's nuts.  It took everything I could do to keep him from coming here and killing you. You have to stop this stuff, Knox.

text: But I love you.
en: But I love you.

text: You say that over and over but you don't even know me!
en: You say that over and over but you don't even know me!

text: Just like that?!  You just knew?
en: Just like that?!  You just knew?

text: Of course just like that.  That's how you always know when it's right.
en: Of course just like that.  That's how you always know when it's right.

text: And if it so happens that you're wrong? If it just so happens that I could care less About you?
en: And if it so happens that you're wrong? If it just so happens that I could care less About you?

text: Then you wouldn't be here warning me about Chet.
en: Then you wouldn't be here warning me about Chet.

text: Are you going with Chet?
en: Are you going with Chet?

text: Chet?  To a play? Are you kidding?
en: Chet?  To a play? Are you kidding?

text: Then come with me.
en: Then come with me.

text: Knox, you are so infuriating!
en: Knox, you are so infuriating!

text: Just give me one chance.  If you don't like me after tonight, I'll stay away forever.
en: Just give me one chance.  If you don't like me after tonight, I'll stay away forever.

text: Uh-huh.
en: Uh-huh.

text: I promise.  Dead Poets honor.  Come with me tonight, then if you don't want to see me again, I swear I'll bow out.
en: I promise.  Dead Poets honor.  Come with me tonight, then if you don't want to see me again, I swear I'll bow out.

text: God, if Chet found out he'd...
en: God, if Chet found out he'd...

text: Chet won't know anything.  We'll sit in back and sneak away as soon as it's over.
en: Chet won't know anything.  We'll sit in back and sneak away as soon as it's over.

text: Knox, if you promise that this will be the end of it-
en: Knox, if you promise that this will be the end of it-

text: Dead Poets honor.
en: Dead Poets honor.

text: What is that?
en: What is that?

text: My Word
en: My Word

text: It's just for a little while. You promised.
en: It's just for a little while. You promised.

text: "We went to the woods because we wanted to suck all the marrow out of life." Anybody want to read?
en: "We went to the woods because we wanted to suck all the marrow out of life." Anybody want to read?

text: I have something.
en: I have something.
